plan.description: Medium-resolution spectral method using tensor product Legendre polynomials
plan._llm_score_rationale: Medium-resolution spectral method offers extremely high accuracy for smooth solutions, with cost justified by the accuracy gain; this is among the most suitable approaches for this problem.

plan.description: Low-resolution spectral method using tensor product Chebyshev polynomials
plan._llm_score_rationale: Spectral methods are ideal for smooth solutions and Dirichlet BCs; even at low resolution, the expected accuracy is very high and the cost is moderate for 5D, making this a strong choice.

plan.description: High-order hp-adaptive finite element method with cubic basis functions
plan._llm_score_rationale: hp-adaptive FEM with cubic basis functions balances high accuracy and efficiency, adapting to solution features; well-suited for smooth, high-dimensional Helmholtz problems, though not as efficient as spectral methods for this analytic solution.
